seed_preparation: Seeds are soaked in water for 24 hours and incubated for sprouting before nursery sowing.
planting_method: Nursery raising followed by transplanting in puddled fields
irrigation_schedule: Continuous shallow flooding (3–5 cm water depth) until flowering stage
harvesting_tips: Harvest when grains are firm and straw turns yellow; avoid over-drying in the field
average_yield: 3–5 tons per hectare (higher in irrigated Terai regions)
plantation_process: Maintain water level and weed control
Apply fertilizer at tillering and panicle stages

seed_preparation: Seeds are cleaned and treated with fungicide before sowing
harvesting_tips: Harvest when grains harden and plants turn golden yellow.

seed_preparation: Seeds are cleaned and treated with fungicide before sowing to prevent fungal diseases.
irrigation_schedule: Irrigation required during knee-high, tasseling, and grain-filling stages if rainfall is insufficient.
harvesting_tips: Harvest when husks turn brown and grains become hard; delay may cause pest damage.
plantation_process: Sow seeds in rows at recommended spacing
